text_fields.name: Cyrus Shekari
text_fields.cards[ba05bbe8-14c8-483b-935d-17c3f30f80:
My SaaS and technology experience is rooted at Microsoft, where I've spent the last couple of years as a Technical Program Manager on Azure. Most of my work has centered on large-scale, multi-team programs that span product, platform, and infrastructure domains - exactly the kind of cross-cutting complexity that defines SaaS delivery at scale. I orchestrated Azure's recovery validation program across 45+ annual resilience drills, driving $14M+ in business impact for enterprise customers like Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp. I also led the 0-to-1 delivery of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and enabled teams to execute with more autonomy.

Beyond delivery, I've been deliberate about using technology to improve how programs run. I built an AI agent for drill planning that cut planning cycle time by 39% and increased drill capacity by 21%. Earlier in my time at Microsoft, I drove adoption of AI-powered code generation workflows across 14 Azure service teams, saving 37 engineering hours monthly. I've also used Power BI dashboards to surface delivery health signals and operational metrics across 140+ teams, giving leadership the visibility to make outcome-aligned investment decisions. Across all of it, the thread has been translating strategy into coordinated execution - managing dependencies, coaching teams on data-driven rhythms, and keeping cross-functional stakeholders aligned on what actually matters to customers.

cover_overrides.label: tell us a bit about your saas and technology experience.

args.label: pointclickcare-8c607ffa

args.label: pointclickcare-8c607ffa
args.comment: If a visible hCaptcha enclave is present, run CaptchaSolver().solve_hcaptcha(...) with the detected sitekey, inject the token, then re-click Submit. If no key is configured, fail this role only and continue the batch.

args.comment: Final post-submit verify (look for confirmation page or error banner).
